content:
# Final Review Planner Positions
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine bugs in the plan_hash auto-population and runless fallback matching logic. Amendments fr-1-0b213bd9 and fr-1-929f7a9f describe the same root cause (unconditional plan_hash auto-population at the service boundary breaks idempotent replay after plan evolution) from complementary angles, and both should be accepted since they propose different (compatible) fix strategies. Amendment fr-1-b656ac14 identifies a real missing started_at guard in the runless fallback path that could cause wrong-attempt finalization.

## Votes

- `fr-1-0b213bd9`: Accept
  The scenario is valid and reproducible: (1) bead completes with plan_hash=None, auto-populated to hash_v1 and stored; (2) persist_plan updates snapshot.plan_hash to hash_v2; (3) idempotent replay of the same completion with plan_hash=None now auto-populates to hash_v2; (4) option_conflicts(Some(hash_v1), Some(hash_v2)) at fs.rs:3161 returns true, triggering a spurious incompatible_terminal_update error. Pre-change behavior (passing None through) was correct because option_conflicts(Some(x), None) is always false, allowing harmless replays. The proposed fixes — either skip auto-population when the entry is already terminal, or weaken the terminal replay conflict check to treat plan_hash mismatch as non-fatal when the stored entry already has a plan_hash (backfill-only semantics) — are both sound approaches. This is a real idempotency violation that would break crash-recovery replay.
- `fr-1-b656ac14`: Accept
  Confirmed by reading the code: at fs.rs:3018, the runless fallback path accepts `[index] if entries[*index].run_id.is_none()` without any started_at validation. Compare with the non-run_id path (line 3077-3081) which explicitly filters by `entries[*index].started_at == started_at`. The service contract at service.rs:464-469 explicitly documents that started_at is forwarded to the lineage layer for deterministic replay of runless completions. Without the started_at check, a completion targeting attempt A (started at time T1) could finalize attempt B (started at time T2) if B happens to be the only open runless entry with the right bead/project. This violates the contract and could corrupt lineage data. Adding `entries[*index].started_at == started_at` as a guard is straightforward and correct.
- `fr-1-929f7a9f`: Accept
  This amendment correctly identifies the same root cause as fr-1-0b213bd9 but focuses on the service-layer fix: the auto-population at service.rs:429-433 and 564-568 is unconditional (`plan_hash.or_else(|| snapshot.plan_hash.clone())`), meaning it replaces None with the *current* snapshot plan_hash before the adapter's matching logic runs. After persist_plan advances snapshot.plan_hash, replays of starts/completions for rows recorded under the old plan will now carry the new hash, tripping the conflict checks at fs.rs:2711-2725 (for starts) and fs.rs:3159-3201 (for completions). The proposed fix — only auto-populate when the matched row lacks plan_hash, not blindly at the service boundary — correctly scopes the backfill to its intended purpose. The request for replay-across-plan-update test coverage is also well-justified since this is a subtle interaction between two features (auto-population and plan evolution) that has no coverage today.
